test.name: Multiple warnings on one person escalate to identity-confusion review
test.description: User asks to check Patrick. The tool returns four warnings: three on Patrick himself (hasEventAfterDeath1 error + hasAgeRangeGreaterThan120 error + tooManyDeathDates2 warning) and one on his relative Thomas (relativesHasAgeRangeGreaterThan120 error). Per the cluster-escalation rule in warnings-as-identity-signals.md, '2+ errors on one person -> almost certainly two people merged.' The skill should NOT just list four warnings independently. It should explicitly recognize the cluster (2 errors + 1 warning ON PATRICK, not counting Thomas's separate relative warning) as a strong identity-confusion signal, separate Thomas's warning into its own treatment, and recommend a timeline review + person_evidence rebuild for Patrick.

input.user_message: Run a thorough warnings check on Patrick Flynn.

judge_context: Should call person_warnings on Patrick (I1) and report all four warnings the tool returned
Should explicitly recognize the cluster of warnings ON PATRICK (2 errors + 1 warning on I1) as a near-certain identity-confusion signal -- not just present four independent items

Should count ONLY Patrick's three warnings toward the cluster verdict, NOT the fourth warning which is on his relative Thomas (I2) -- relativesHasAgeRangeGreaterThan120 is a separate concern about the parent-child link, not another strike against Patrick
Should treat Thomas's relative-warning per the relative-warning rule: recommend verifying the parent-child link via person-evidence BEFORE recommending data fixes on Thomas
Should hand off to the timeline skill to find Patrick's split point, AND to person-evidence to rebuild the assertion-to-person links once the split is identified
Should classify the severities correctly: hasEventAfterDeath1, hasAgeRangeGreaterThan120, and relativesHasAgeRangeGreaterThan120 as Critical (error); tooManyDeathDates2 as Note (warning)
Should NOT recommend fixing individual fields on Patrick when the cluster suggests the whole profile is the wrong thing -- fixing one date won't clear the underlying identity issue
Should reference the escalation rule from warnings-as-identity-signals.md (2+ errors -> split the profile, rebuild person_evidence)
